# Content of the article
In this Blog I will explain the code of my simple Letterboxed-comment scraper.

# Code

## Loading librarys

- rvest is necessary for scraping
- tidyverse is used for pipeing data efficiently and because it loads stringr for cleaningpurposes


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
library(rvest)
library(tidyverse)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}
```
── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
✔ dplyr     1.1.3     ✔ readr     2.1.4
✔ forcats   1.0.0     ✔ stringr   1.5.0
✔ ggplot2   3.4.4     ✔ tibble    3.2.1
✔ lubridate 1.9.3     ✔ tidyr     1.3.0
✔ purrr     1.0.2     
── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
✖ dplyr::filter()         masks stats::filter()
✖ readr::guess_encoding() masks rvest::guess_encoding()
✖ dplyr::lag()            masks stats::lag()
ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
```
:::
:::


## scraping function explained

Webpages consist of a html framework. To  view or download it by hand rightclick on a page and "Show page Source text". So when understanding how to download webpages it's necessary to at least learn the very basics of html (wich isn't that hard, trust me). 

I wont explain HTML here, this is a scraping tutorial. If you dont know anything about html go on Youtube, watch a short introvideo and come back.
